creator: ghostp@dzsy.org
text:
Gavin Atkinson wrote:
> On Wed, 2009-03-11 at 16:31 +0100, Székvölgyi Péter wrote:
>   
>> gavin@FreeBSD.org wrote:
>>     
>>> To submitter: have you loaded the acpi_asus module?
>>>
>>>       
>> Yes, I tested with all notebook modell kernel modul, nothing changed.
>>     
>
> Could you please provide the output of the "dmesg" and "devinfo -rv"
> commands?
>   
I booted my mashine with verbose mode, and if a want to change my brightness
i saw in dmesg ( brightness down with key ):
acpi: bad write to port 0x080 (32), val 0x1d
or ( brightness up with key )
acpi: bad write to port 0x080 (32), val 0x1c

I didn't load acpi_asus at this time.

I tested with other dsdt file from google acer acpi, nothing changed:
http://aceracpi.googlecode.com/svn/trunk/dsdt/acer/extensa/

If you want you can see the dmesg with verbose mode on this url:
http://phoenix.dzsy.org/~ghostp/dmesg.out
and devinfo -rv:
http://phoenix.dzsy.org/~ghostp/devinfo.out

I install FreeBSD-Current to an usb key and booted. The error is the 
same with
brightness control.

Now my system is:
FreeBSD dzsy-note.dzsy.org 7.2-STABLE FreeBSD 7.2-STABLE #0: Sat May 23 
01:25:19 CEST 2009     
root@dzsy-note.dzsy.org:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/DZSYNOTE  i386

Thanks your help!

Best regards,
Peter
